content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The client ends the conversation in essentially the same stance they started: composed, articulate, intellectually curious, and unmoved. Across all seven client turns, the language stays in explanatory/diagnostic mode ('logistical thing', 'task switching is hard', 'ADHD stuff', 'just a problem I haven't solved yet', 'it's fine', 'I'm pretty comfortable with uncertainty'). There is no somatic arrival, no quality of 'fine' becoming felt, no 'huh' moment, no quieting or softening. When the coach asks about feeling (turn 8) and sitting with not knowing (turn 10), the client deflects with 'nothing, really' and 'it's fine' — exactly the numbness layer the breakthrough_condition targets — but the coach never names or stays with the texture of 'fine'; the client therefore never notices that 'fine' has a quality. The conversation is still pre-shift at turn 14, with the client asking the coach what they're thinking. The breakthrough condition (client notices 'fine' has a flatness, a not-quite-here-ness) was approached in turn 11 but not entered.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: 'fear isn't really my deal', framing as logistics/discipline. Surface presentation intact."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Still composed: 'probably fine', 'we always do'. No movement, slight smile but no shift."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Self-amused but still in problem-solving frame: 'focus thing', 'better system'."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client lands a tidy ADHD/complexity explanation — clear sign of the happiness filter / explanatory armor reasserting itself."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Direct opportunity for numbness to be noticed: 'nothing, really... I was just answering the question.' The numbness surfaces but is not stayed with."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "'It's fine. A little frustrating maybe.' Classic 'fine' that the breakthrough requires the coach to slow down on — but the texture is not explored, and the client closes back up ('settles back slightly')."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client turns the inquiry back on the coach, still even-toned and curious-but-untouched. No softening, no somatic shift."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-happiness-filter", "disassociating-in-the-coaching-room", "lets-go-there", "fear-in-body-vs-fear-in-head", "how-is-my-anxiety-right-now", "disassociation-as-numbness-layer"],
  "notes": "Coach avoided the major traps (didn't argue fearlessness, didn't push fear, didn't diagnose) but never asked what 'fine' actually feels like or oriented to the body, so the numbness layer surfaced verbally (turns 9, 11) without becoming felt. Conversation may be cut off mid-arc, but as scored, no shift is visible in client turns."
}
